title: Aureline M1 docs/help/About/service-health truth-source badge-vocabulary seed
description: Boundary schema for artifacts/help/m1_truth_source_examples.yaml. The seed is the canonical, machine-readable source of the closed badge vocabulary that Help, About, service-health, and the docs/help browser project for provenance, freshness, client scope, install mode, service health, and source class. Each row pins one badge family (source class, version-match state, freshness class, client-scope, install mode, provenance row state, or service-health state), the closed token vocabulary the surface MUST render verbatim, the explicit honesty-fallback token (so the surface never blanks out a row), the consuming surface classes that MUST stay parity-consistent, and a named runtime consumer (a docs page, the live shell module, support-export copy, or a CI validator). Rows MUST also pin one canonical example payload under fixtures/help/m1_truth_source_examples/. Adding a new badge family is additive-minor and bumps schema_version; repurposing an existing badge_family_class, surface_class, or vocabulary_role token is breaking and requires a new decision row plus a superseding seed.

schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this seed. Bumped only on breaking envelope changes.

required_badge_family_class_coverage.description: Every required badge_family_class MUST appear in at least one row so help/About/service-health/docs-browser surfaces all have a frozen badge family in this seed.

required_consuming_surface_class_coverag.description: Help / About / service-health / docs-browser MUST appear on every row so no consuming surface can fork private wording from the seed.

badge_family_class.description: Closed badge-family vocabulary. Each family pins one row of closed tokens that Help/About/service-health/docs-browser MUST quote verbatim.

vocabulary_role_class.description: Closed role vocabulary so a row can label which tokens are healthy, which are honesty fallbacks, which are degraded states, and which are seed placeholders awaiting wiring.

consuming_surface_class.description: Closed surface vocabulary mirrored from the help/About/service-health/docs-browser audit-packet contract so this seed and the audit packet read the same surface classes.

consumer_class.description: Closed consumer vocabulary so every row names a real reader of the seed rather than a marketing surface.

stable_dot_id.description: Stable dot-prefixed badge-family row id (for example 'truth_source.docs_help_source_class').

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence safe for docs, packets, and the validation lane capture.

token.description: Stable snake_case token the surface renders verbatim. The surface MUST NOT invent surface-local synonyms.

consumer_ref.description: Repo-relative path of the named runtime consumer. The validation lane resolves this on disk.

forced_input.description: Forced overrides the runner replays under --force-drill to reproduce the named drift.

vocabulary_tokens.description: Closed token vocabulary the consuming surfaces MUST render verbatim. Tokens carry a role so honesty fallbacks, degraded states, and seed placeholders are explicit rather than implicit.

frozen_token_count.description: The locked count of tokens in vocabulary_tokens. Adding or removing a token without bumping this count is a structural mismatch and fails truth_source.vocabulary_token_count_mismatch; bumping the count requires landing the new token in the badge vocabulary draft and example payload at the same time.

honesty_fallback_token.description: The single token a consuming surface renders when upstream truth is missing or unverified. MUST also appear in vocabulary_tokens with role 'honesty_fallback_token'.

consuming_surface_classes.description: Surfaces that MUST consume this row's vocabulary. help_pane, about_pane, service_health_pane, and docs_browser_pane MUST appear on every row so the seed never permits a consuming surface to fork.

support_export_compatible.description: Per the contract goal, every badge family in the seed MUST be safe for support / export copy without surface-local rewrites. The constant is structural.

example_payload_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the canonical example payload the validation lane parses end-to-end.
